title: Coding Prompts, Scoped Edits, and Regression Control · Transfer Evaluation
summary: Implement a repository change with tests and minimal unrelated churn. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a coding-agent implementation brief with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.
Ground implementation in repository commands, architecture, dependency policy, and local instructions.
Specify risk-proportionate tests and observable regression checks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
Use a changed lab case derived from this module: prompt coding edits. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed lab case derived from this module: prompt coding edits. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. What must be established before implementation?

content.title: Worked case pattern

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-implementation-evidence-1tk9ad" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Implementation Evidence into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-implementation-evidence-1tk9ad
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

stimulus.title: Implementation Evidence failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A code diff is sufficient proof that a change works.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Implementation Evidence condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Implementation Evidence applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.
